violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety certified with RIDOH. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The hand wash sink in the bar area is lacking adequate signage. The currently has two 3-bays in the bar area. A sink must be designated as hand wash only sink in the bar area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was placed in dish washer. Ice machine taken out of service and ice was disposed of. Ice machine cleaning frequency to be increased. Ice machine to not be used until cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
Boxes of food containers were observed stored on the floor. Food/food contact surfaces must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3-bay. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2024
Allergy statement is lacking on the menus. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Application printed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of bio-film. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off. Ice was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Steak is served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Meat and fish were observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Food was relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils were relocated to hot hold. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
The hand sink in the kitchen was lacking hot water. System must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety registered with RIDOH. Application printed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of bio-film. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off. Ice was disposed of. PIC stated, ice machine was cleaned. PIC to examine ice machine before turning back on. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety registered with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food peppers with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Food was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by various equipment. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
The date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Handout provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Water booster was turned off. Booster was turned back on. Booster should always be on when washing dishes. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Various foods in the mini fridge was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Unit was adjusted to allow food to maintain adequate temperature. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Meat was observed thawing in a bucket in the 3-bay sink in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Food was moved to walk-In. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Proper holding procedures reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of bio-film. Ice machine to be turned off and washed rinsed and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
